system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet straight duct (along +x)
    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (  0  25 -25)   // 1
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 3
    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5 (same as 1)
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6 (same as 2)
    (-50 -25  25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend roof connecting block 0 to bend
    (  0  25  25)   // 8 (same as 4)
    (  0  25 -25)   // 9 (same as 5/1)
    ( 25  25 -25)   // 10
    ( 25  25  25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - bend transition (goes down in -y)
    (  0 -25  25)   // 12 (same as 7)
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 13 (same as 6/2)
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 14
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 15

    // Block 3 - outlet straight duct extending in -y
    (  0 -125  25)  // 16
    (  0 -125 -25)  // 17
    ( 25 -125 -25)  // 18
    ( 25 -125  25)  // 19
    (-500 -50  25)  // 20
    (-500 -50 -25)  // 21
    (-500 -125 -25) // 22
    (-500 -125  25)  // 23
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight duct (along +x)
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend roof region
    hex (4 5 10 11 7 6 13 12) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Post-bend transition
    hex (14 15 19 18 13 12 16 17) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Outlet duct
    hex (20 23 22 21 16 17 23 22) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (20 21 22 23)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 1 5 4)   // front wall (+z)
            (0 3 2 1)   // back wall (-z)
            (1 2 6 5)   // left wall (-y)
            (4 5 6 7)   // right wall (+y)

            // Block 1 walls
            (4 8 11 7)  // left wall (-y)
            (5 6 10 9)  // right wall (+y)
            (8 9 10 11) // front wall (+z)
            (8 11 9 10) // back wall (-z)

            // Block 2 walls
            (12 13 16 17) // back wall (-z)
            (14 15 19 18) // front wall (+z)
            (12 13 14 15) // left wall (-y)
            (16 17 18 19) // right wall (+y)

            // Block 3 walls
            (20 21 23 22) // front wall (+z)
            (20 16 17 21) // back wall (-z)
            (20 23 17 22) // left wall (-y)
            (21 22 17 16) // right wall (+y)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //
')]</foamfiles>

    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

Block hex (14 15 19 18 13 12 16 17) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) is inside-out

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_20260516_195157_run2/runs/squareBend/1/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 56.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are two errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (14 15 19 18 13 12 16 17) ... is inside-out`
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading from blockMesh failure)

## Error 1 Deep Dive

The error is:
```
Block hex (14 15 19 18 13 12 16 17) ... is inside-out
```

This means the vertex ordering violates the right-hand rule — the normals point inward instead of outward. Looking at the current block definitions:

- **Block 2**: `hex (14 15 19 18 13 12 16 17)`

Let me trace the vertices:
- Bottom face: 14=(25,-50,-25), 15=(25,-50,25), 19=(25,-125,25), 18=(25,-125,-25) — all at x=25! This is a **degenerate face** (a line/plane, not a proper quadrilateral).
- Top face: 13=(25,-75,-25), 12=(25,-75,25), 16=(0,-125,25), 17=(0,-125,-25)

The fundamental problem revealed in Attempt 4 is that **vertices 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19** all lie on planes where x=0 or x=25. The block definition `hex (14 15 19 18 13 12 16 17)` connects vertices that are coplanar in the bottom face, creating a degenerate/inverted hexahedron.

### Root Cause

The block topology is fundamentally flawed. Vertices 12-19 form a "stair-step" region, but the current block definitions either create degenerate faces or inward-pointing normals. The core issue is that the geometry tries to represent a sharp L-bend with blocks that don't form valid convex hexahedra.

### Recommended Solution

Given that previous attempts at manual block reordering have repeatedly failed, **restructure the block topology to use fewer, well-proven blocks**. The simplest and most reliable approach:

1. **Merge Blocks 1 and 2** into a single larger block that covers the entire bend region from x=0 to x=25 and y=-50 to y=25. Define it as:
   ```
   hex (4 5 10 11 7 6 13 12) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
   ```
   This is the same as the current Block 1 — keep it.

2. **Define Block 2** as the post-bend duct going in -y from y=-50 to y=-125 at x=25:
   ```
   hex (12 13 15 14 16 17 18 19) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
   ```

3. **Define Block 3** as the long outlet duct from y=-50 to y=-125 at x=-500:
   ```
   hex (20 21 22 23 16 17 18 19) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
   ```

4. **Critical fix**: Reverse the vertex ordering of **Block 2**:
   ```
   hex (14 15 19 18 13 12 16 17)
   ```
   becomes
   ```
   hex (19 18 14 15 17 16 13 12)
   ```

   Or more systematically, try:
   ```
   hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19)
   ```

   With the bottom face `(12 13 14 15)` and top face `(16 17 18 19)`. Verify the right-hand rule: edges (13-12)×(14-12) should point roughly toward decreasing y, and the normal should align with the block's interior-to-exterior direction.

5. **Verify all boundary faces** in the `walls` patch are consistent with the new block topology.

## Error 2

This is a cascading error. Once blockMesh succeeds, this resolves automatically.

## Summary

1. **Fix Block 2 vertex ordering** in `blockMeshDict` to `hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19)` — placing the "lower-y" vertices (12,13,14,15 at y=-75,-50) as the bottom face and higher-y vertices (16,17,18,19 at y=-125) as the top face, ensuring the right-hand rule produces outward-pointing normals.

2. **Update all boundary face definitions** in `blockMeshDict` to match the corrected block topology.

3. **Re-run `blockMesh`** before attempting `rhoSimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change Block 2 hex definition from hex (14 15 19 18 13 12 16 17) to hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19) with same grading (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1); Update Block 3 hex definition from hex (20 21 22 23 16 17 23 22) to hex (20 21 22 23 16 17 18 19) with same grading (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1); Remove duplicate entry 23 in Block 3 top face since vertex 23 already exists; Keep all other blocks unchanged'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
